title: Refine the delivered browse surface: renderable detail hero, tag counts, multi-tag filtering, wide-screen layout
summary: Four refinements to the shipped browse surface. The detail page's hero switches from streaming the raw original (which breaks on iPhone HEIC in desktop browsers) to a new mid-size JPEG derivative served on its own media route. The tag chip rail gains a muted global photo count on every chip. The home filter grows AND-semantics multi-tag support (?tag=a&tag=b) with add/remove chip interaction and a tag-set-carrying load-more cursor. The whole surface finally uses wide screens: a centered content column, a wrapping chip rail, and an auto-fill grid, all in CSS alone.
motivation: Opening an iPhone photo today renders a broken hero image and drags full original bytes through the tunnel; the fix also cuts detail-page weight. Counts on chips answer "how much is under this tag" without a click. Multi-tag AND filtering makes the catalog findable once tags overlap. And the delivered phone-width layout wastes every desktop viewport — a pure-CSS column, wrap, and auto-fill grid fixes that without touching templates.

included: internal/thumb + internal/web/media.go — a size-parameterized 1280px JPEG preview derivative through the existing vips shell-out seam, served on a new Access-wrapped /media/previews/{id} route; ThumbPurger purge extended to remove all cached size variants

internal/store + internal/web chip rail — a global per-tag count query (GROUP BY over photo_tags) replacing ListTags for the rail, rendered as a muted count span on every tag chip plus a count-free All chip

internal/store + internal/web home route — ListPhotos/CountPhotos take a []string tag set with AND semantics (dynamic ? placeholder list only, INV-004; every element through the single normalizeTag path, INV-005); chip hrefs add/remove tags from the active set; load-more URL carries the full set

internal/web/assets/app.css + css_test.go — responsive wide-screen rules only: centered ~1080px browse column, wrapping chip rail, repeat(auto-fill, minmax(168px, 1fr)) grid, ~720px detail column with capped hero, snapbar aligned to the column
excluded: Any change to GET /media/originals/{id} or the stored original bytes — the originals route and the Open-original link stay byte-for-byte unchanged (INV-002)
Any change to the upload/capture flow, EXIF handling, or the delete route's semantics (only its purge coverage extends)
OR-semantics or any filter combinator beyond AND; per-filter-conditioned chip counts (counts are always global)
Tag garbage collection or any tag-model reshaping — orphaned tags stay and render with count 0
New design tokens, new env vars, new dependencies, or any Go/templ structural change for the wide-screen item (pure CSS is the contract)
Any change to internal/store/schema.sql or migrations

design_facts.source: https://claude.ai/design/p/48eada1e-cbaf-40d2-8b93-308d71b2f564 — delivered hybrid vocabulary extended; comps for this slice shared in-session 2026-07-17
design_facts.stack: Go 1.26 + templ/HTMX + modernc SQLite; one static CGO-free binary

token_manifest.notes: No new tokens. The delivered hybrid vocabulary in internal/web/assets/app.css is reused as-is; the wide-screen item adds only layout rules (media queries), never color/type tokens.

work_items.title: Serve a 1280px JPEG preview derivative and point the detail hero at it
work_items.summary: Adds a size-parameterized variant of the existing vips thumbnail path (longest edge 1280, same cache dir with a size-suffixed key, same singleflight/bounded/placeholder behavior), exposes it on GET /media/previews/{id} inside the Access-wrapped mux, switches the detail hero to it, and extends Purge plus the WI29-era tests to remove ALL cached size variants on delete. The originals route and Open-original link stay byte-for-byte unchanged.

contract.generation: internal/thumb gains an exported GeneratePreview(ctx, id, ext string) ([]byte, bool, error) on *Generator: the SAME vips `thumbnail` subprocess pipeline as Generate (same Runner seam, same temp-file flow, same vipsTimeout, same 0o700 cache dir, same atomic rename write) with the size argument "1280" instead of "512" — the size is the ONLY pipeline difference. Return triple semantics are identical to Generate: (bytes, false, nil) real from vips or disk cache; (placeholder, true, nil) embedded placeholder on vips absence/failure; (nil, false, err wrapping fs.ErrNotExist) for an unknown storage key. The preview's singleflight key incorporates the size (e.g. "1280|"+id) so a 512 and a 1280 generation for the same id never dedupe into each other. *thumb.Bounded implements GeneratePreview by acquiring the SAME semaphore as Generate, then delegating verbatim — one shared vips concurrency bound.
contract.caching: Same disk cache directory (GRINGOTTS_THUMB_CACHE_DIR — no new env var, min.3). The preview cache key incorporates the size: fmt.Sprintf("%x-1280.jpg", sha256(id)); the existing 512 key ("%x.jpg") stays byte-identical so already-cached thumbnails survive untouched and the two variants coexist on disk. The HTTP response reuses the existing cacheControl const ("public, max-age=31536000, immutable") and Content-Type image/jpeg.
contract.purge: (*Generator).Purge(id) removes ALL cached size variants for the id: the 512 path AND the 1280 path, as two deterministic filepath.Join + os.Remove calls — still no globbing, walking, or ReadDir. Per file, fs.ErrNotExist collapses to nil; any other removal error surfaces unmodified. purge.go's single-file doc comment is rewritten to the multi-variant contract. internal/thumb/purge_test.go (WI29-era) extends to seed BOTH variants and assert both are removed while another id's variants survive, and internal/web/delete_test.go's cache assertions extend so DELETE /photos/{id} provably clears both variants.
contract.routing: The web.ThumbGenerator seam gains GeneratePreview (same signature shape); *thumb.Generator and *thumb.Bounded both satisfy it, so composition-root wiring in cmd/gringotts is unchanged. MediaHandler gains handlePreview serving GET /media/previews/{id} — the route name follows the existing plural /media/ pattern — registered inside NewServer on the same single mux, so the composition root's whole-mux Access wrap gates it (INV-009) and the specific pattern wins over GET / under Go 1.22 precedence. Status mapping mirrors handleThumbnail exactly: store.GetPhoto resolves FIRST (unknown id → 404 via WriteHeader, before any generation runs), any generate error → 404 with no Content-Type, success → 200. The test-only RegisterMedia shim mounts the new route alongside the existing two.
contract.template: internal/web/detail.templ's hero src switches from templ.SafeURL("/media/originals/" + v.ID) to templ.SafeURL("/media/previews/" + v.ID). The Open-original href stays "/media/originals/" + v.ID byte-identical, and handleOriginal in media.go is untouched. detail_templ.go is regenerated and committed. No new CSS class or token.
contract.invariants: INV-002: the originals route, the stored bytes, and the save path are untouched; all pixel decode stays in internal/thumb. INV-003: vips only ever via exec subprocess — no cgo, no linked libraries; CGO_ENABLED=0 build holds. INV-009: the new route rides the existing Access-wrapped mux; no second mux, no bypass. No new config knob, env var, or dependency (min.1/min.3).

oracle.command: go run github.com/a-h/templ/cmd/templ@v0.3.1020 generate && go vet ./... && go test ./... && CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts
assertions: GET /media/previews/{id} for a seeded photo (stub Runner) returns 200 with Content-Type image/jpeg and Cache-Control "public, max-age=31536000, immutable", and the runner received vips `thumbnail` args carrying "1280" as the size argument — the 512 route's args are unchanged.
An unknown id returns 404 with no Content-Type and the runner is never invoked (DB-first fail-closed ordering); a failing/absent vips serves the embedded placeholder at 200, never an error status.

Cache variants coexist: after generating both the thumbnail and the preview for one id, the cache dir holds two distinct files — sha256hex + ".jpg" and the size-suffixed "-1280.jpg" — and a repeat preview request is served from disk without re-invoking the runner; concurrent same-id preview requests dedupe via singleflight without colliding with a concurrent 512 generation.

Purge(id) removes BOTH cached size variants for the id and leaves another id's variants intact; purging with neither variant present returns nil; a non-ENOENT removal error surfaces; the extended internal/web delete-flow test proves DELETE /photos/{id} clears both variants from the cache dir.

GET /photos/{id} renders the hero with src="/media/previews/{id}" while the Open-original link href stays "/media/originals/{id}", and GET /media/originals/{id} still streams the stored bytes byte-identical with the stored original_format Content-Type — INV-002 untouched by diff and by test.

GET /media/previews/{id} is registered inside NewServer's single mux (server_media_routes_test.go extended), so the composition root's whole-mux Access middleware gates it exactly like the existing media routes (INV-009).
The full CI gate passes: templ generate leaves no diff, go vet ./... and go test ./... are clean, and CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts succeeds.

work_items.title: Show each tag's global photo count on its rail chip
work_items.summary: Replaces ListTags with a name-plus-global-count query (GROUP BY over photo_tags, ORDER BY name ASC), renders the count as a muted aria-hidden span after each chip name, and introduces the count-free All chip at the head of the rail. Chip href semantics stay single-tag in this item; counts are never conditioned on the active filter.
work_items.design_reference: / — tag chip rail with count badges (comps shared in-session 2026-07-17)

contract.store: internal/store/list.go replaces ListTags with ListTagCounts(ctx, db) ([]TagCount, error), TagCount being struct{ Name string; Count int }. One query, no combinatorics: SELECT t.name, COUNT(pt.photo_id) FROM tags t LEFT JOIN photo_tags pt ON pt.tag_id = t.id GROUP BY t.id, t.name ORDER BY t.name ASC — LEFT JOIN so an orphaned tag (tolerated in this catalog) renders with count 0. Counts are GLOBAL, never conditioned on the active filter. Non-nil empty slice when no tags, never an error; the query takes no user input, and any future bind stays a ? placeholder (INV-004). ListTags is deleted — handleCapturePage is its only caller — and its list_test.go coverage migrates to ListTagCounts (deletion over addition, minimalism). The stale doc-comment mentions of ListTags (internal/store/count.go, internal/web/capture.go, and the chiprail doc comment) are updated in the same sweep.
contract.view_model: internal/web/capture.go: browseData.Tags becomes []tagChip, tagChip being struct{ Name string; Count string } assembled in handleCapturePage — Count is the PRE-FORMATTED display string (strconv.Itoa of the global count), so templ receives display-ready strings and all formatting lives in Go (go-htmx htmx.8). No other browseData field changes; Active stays the single normalized tag string in this item.
contract.template:
chiprail's signature becomes chiprail(chips []tagChip, active string); capture.templ's call site updates and both *_templ.go files are regenerated and committed. Rail markup, stated precisely: len(chips)==0 renders NOTHING (unchanged empty state). Otherwise
opens with the All chip — All when active=="", else All — which NEVER carries a count (the header count already shows totals). Each tag chip then renders its name followed immediately by {Count} (aria-hidden so the count never joins the link's accessible text): inactive — {name}… with tagHref unchanged; active — {name}…×. Href semantics are UNCHANGED in this item (single-tag add on inactive via /?tag=name, clear-to-/ on active) — multi-tag hrefs are the next item's scope. Every rail element stays an , never a
contract.styling: internal/web/assets/app.css gains exactly one new rule, .chip-count — muted register via the EXISTING var(--muted) and a smaller font-size; no new custom property, color, or type token (token_manifest). The rule lands BEFORE the final form.htmx-request .snapbtn busy hook, which remains the last rule in the file and keeps owning the last .snapbtn occurrence. css_test.go extends with a .chip-count presence assertion; the position guard keeps passing.
contract.invariants: Counts are global — one aggregate query per page render, never filter-conditioned. INV-004 (parameterized SQL), INV-005 untouched (no new normalization site; the rail still labels chips only from stored names — the raw ?tag= query is never echoed). No new dependency, no new config.

oracle.command: go run github.com/a-h/templ/cmd/templ@v0.3.1020 generate && go vet ./... && go test ./... && CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts
assertions: Store: over a seeded catalog (one tag linked to two photos, one to one photo, one orphaned tag linked to none) ListTagCounts returns rows in name-ascending order with counts 2, 1, and 0 — the orphaned tag present with count 0 — and no ListTags identifier remains in any .go or .templ source (archived docs under docs/exec-plans/completed/ keep their history).

GET / renders each tag chip as its stored name followed immediately by N inside the , N being the tag's GLOBAL count; the rail opens with the All chip (All when unfiltered) and the All chip carries no chip-count span.

GET /?tag=x keeps single-tag behavior byte-for-byte in structure: x's chip renders class "chip chip-on" with href="/" and the trailing × chip-x span, its chip-count span still present between name and dismiss glyph; the All chip renders without chip-on; every chip's count equals its unfiltered value (counts never conditioned on the active filter).
A tagless catalog renders no <nav class="chiprail"> at all (unchanged empty state), and the rendered page still contains exactly one <button> (the capture submit).

Served /assets/app.css contains a .chip-count rule using var(--muted) and introducing no new custom property; form.htmx-request .snapbtn remains the FINAL rule and owns the last .snapbtn occurrence in the file — css_test.go's position guard and the new presence assertion pass.
The full CI gate passes: templ generate leaves no diff, go vet ./... and go test ./... are clean, and CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts succeeds.

work_items.title: Filter the catalog by multiple tags with AND semantics
work_items.summary: GET /?tag=a&tag=b shows photos carrying ALL given tags. ListPhotos and CountPhotos take a []string tag set (every element through the single normalizeTag path; dynamic ? placeholder lists only; GROUP BY / HAVING equality for AND). Inactive chips add their tag to the set, active chips remove theirs, the All chip clears, the header reads K of N, and the load-more cursor carries the full set. Single-tag URLs behave identically to today.
work_items.design_reference: /?tag=a&tag=b — chip rail add/remove interaction, K-of-N header, intersection grid (comps shared in-session 2026-07-17)

contract.store: ListPhotos(ctx, db, tags []string, before string) and CountPhotos(ctx, db, tags []string) replace the single-tag signatures; handleCapturePage and handlePhotosFragment are the only callers and update in this item. Inside the store, every element passes through normalizeTag — the single normalization path, INV-005 — elements normalizing to "" are dropped, and the normalized set is DEDUPLICATED preserving first-appearance order (a duplicate would break the HAVING equality). An empty resulting set means NO filter — exactly today's tag=="" branch. The filtered shape: JOIN photo_tags pt / tags t, WHERE t.name IN (?, …) with the placeholder list built ONLY as a dynamically repeated "?" string — values always bound, never interpolated (INV-004) — GROUP BY p.id HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.name) = ? bound to len(set), giving AND semantics. Unknown (never-stored) normalized tags STAY in the set — fail-closed: the HAVING equality can then never hold, so the result is an empty page / count 0, never an error and never a silent widening to fewer tags. Keyset contract unchanged: before=="" is first-page, non-empty is an opaque `id < ?` bound, PageSize cap, non-nil empty slices.
contract.routing: handleCapturePage and handlePhotosFragment read the FULL multi-value query — r.URL.Query()["tag"] — and forward the raw slice verbatim to the store (the handlers never trim or lowercase; active-chip matching uses store.NormalizeTag per element, the same exported helper as today). GET /?tag=a behaves identically to the pre-change single-tag page and GET / identically to the unfiltered page (backward compatible). Duplicate, unknown, and whitespace-only tag values return 200, never 500.
contract.view_model: browseData.Active becomes the normalized, deduplicated active set ([]string, first-appearance order); IsFiltered means len(Active) > 0. Filtered (K) is CountPhotos over the WHOLE active set — the intersection count — and the header keeps the existing pre-formatted strings: fmt.Sprintf("%d of %d", K, N) when filtered, fmt.Sprintf("%d photos", N) otherwise (htmx.8: formatting stays in Go).
contract.template: chiprail(chips []tagChip, active []string): the All chip keeps href="/" and is chip-on iff len(active)==0. An INACTIVE chip's href ADDS its tag — url.Values with q["tag"] = active set plus the chip's name → "/?"+q.Encode(), multi-value tag params, always escaped, never concatenated. An ACTIVE chip renders chip-on with the × chip-x dismiss span (shared term chip-dismiss-glyph) and its href REMOVES that tag, keeping the rest in set order — the remaining set's multi-value URL, or "/" when it was the last active tag. The chip-count span from tag-chip-counts renders identically on every chip (shared terms chip-count-span, all-chip-label). Both *_templ.go files regenerated and committed.
contract.pagination: LoadMoreURL (full first page) and handlePhotosFragment's self-perpetuating trigger URL carry the FULL tag set — one tag param per element via url.Values — plus before=<oldest id>; the fragment handler round-trips the same set so every subsequent page filters identically.
contract.invariants: INV-004: only the ? placeholder list is ever built dynamically — never a value. INV-005: every element of every filter path (list and count) passes through the store's single normalizeTag. INV-009: no new route — semantics change rides the existing Access-wrapped GET / and GET /photos. No change to detail, upload, delete, or media routes, and no schema/migration change.

oracle.command: go run github.com/a-h/templ/cmd/templ@v0.3.1020 generate && go vet ./... && go test ./... && CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts
assertions: Store AND semantics: with photos A{a,b}, B{a}, C{b}, ListPhotos with tags {"a","b"} returns only A and CountPhotos returns 1; a single-element set {"a"} returns exactly the pre-change single-tag results; an empty (or all-whitespace) set returns the unfiltered page and total.

INV-005 and INV-004 on every filter path: raw elements like " A " match the stored "a" through the store's single normalizeTag in BOTH ListPhotos and CountPhotos; duplicates ("a","a") dedupe so the HAVING equality still matches; the IN clause binds every value through ? placeholders with only the placeholder list built dynamically (asserted against the query text / by a mixed-case-with-quote tag value that would break interpolation).

GET /?tag=a&tag=b returns 200 with the header reading "K of N" where K is the intersection count, the grid holding only intersection cells; GET /?tag=a renders identically to the pre-change single-tag page; GET /?tag=a&tag=a collapses to the single-tag result and GET /?tag=zzz (unknown) renders an empty grid with "0 of N" — never a 500.

Rail interaction with active {a}: inactive chip b's href is the escaped multi-value URL carrying tag=a AND tag=b; active chip a renders chip-on with the × dismiss span and href "/" (last active tag) — with active {a,b}, a's href carries only tag=b; the All chip href is "/" and is chip-on only when no tag is active; every chip keeps its chip-count span byte-identical to the counts item's markup.

Load-more carries the set: a full first page under active {a,b} renders a trigger URL containing before=<oldest id> plus BOTH tag params; GET /photos with that URL returns the next intersection-only page and, when full, re-emits a trigger preserving the full tag set.
The full CI gate passes: templ generate leaves no diff, go vet ./... and go test ./... are clean, and CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts succeeds.

work_items.title: Use wide viewports: centered column, wrapping rail, auto-fill grid — CSS only
work_items.summary: Adds responsive rules to app.css so the browse surface centers in a ~1080px column with a wrapping chip rail and an auto-fill photo grid, and the detail page centers at ~720px with a height-capped hero — zero Go/templ change. New rules land before the busy hook, which stays the final rule; css_test.go's served-CSS assertions extend to cover the new rules.
work_items.design_reference: / and /photos/{id} at wide viewports — centered-column comps shared in-session 2026-07-17

contract.styling: All changes land in internal/web/assets/app.css as layout rules inside min-width media queries; every rule targets an EXISTING class — no selector renames, no new color/type token (token_manifest), and zero Go/templ structural change is the contract (if implementation proves a templ change unavoidable, the item fails its contract and returns to planning; none is anticipated). The rules: (1) a wide-viewport media query widens the browse column — main/.capture max-width to ~1080px (the base 480px rule is untouched); (2) .chiprail switches to flex-wrap: wrap with horizontal scrolling disabled at wide widths, so chips flow onto multiple rows; (3) .grid switches to grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fill, minmax(168px, 1fr)); (4) .detail constrains to a centered ~720px column and the .detail-photo img gains a max-height cap (~70vh with object-fit: contain) so the 1280px hero never towers; (5) .snapbar's content aligns to the same centered column (it already nests inside main.capture, so the rule keeps its sticky bar visually on-column at the new width). Below the breakpoints the emitted base CSS is byte-unchanged — mobile rendering is untouched.
contract.ordering: Every new rule lands BEFORE the form.htmx-request .snapbtn busy hook, which remains the FINAL rule in the file and keeps owning the last .snapbtn occurrence — css_test.go's existing position guard must keep passing unmodified in meaning.
contract.tests: internal/web/css_test.go extends its served-CSS string assertions (the existing fetch-and-strip style): presence of the wide-viewport media query with the ~1080px browse column, the .chiprail wrap rule, the literal repeat(auto-fill, minmax(168px, 1fr)), the ~720px detail column, and the hero max-height cap — plus a re-assertion that the busy hook is still last.
contract.invariants: Pure CSS: the diff touches only app.css and css_test.go; rendered HTML for GET / and GET /photos/{id} is byte-identical to before the item. No new custom property, no new class beyond what layout requires (target existing classes; introduce none), no new dependency.

oracle.command: go run github.com/a-h/templ/cmd/templ@v0.3.1020 generate && go vet ./... && go test ./... && CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts
assertions: Served /assets/app.css contains min-width media-query rules for: the ~1080px centered browse column on the existing main/.capture selectors, .chiprail flex-wrap: wrap, .grid repeat(auto-fill, minmax(168px, 1fr)), the ~720px centered .detail column, and a .detail-photo img max-height cap — each asserted as a literal in css_test.go's stripped-CSS style.
All new rules sit inside media queries and no base (narrow-viewport) rule's declarations change — the phone-width output path is byte-unchanged outside the new query blocks.
form.htmx-request .snapbtn remains the FINAL rule in the file and owns the last .snapbtn occurrence — the existing position guard passes with every new rule landing before it.

The item's diff touches only internal/web/assets/app.css and internal/web/css_test.go: templ generate leaves no diff and the rendered HTML of GET / and GET /photos/{id} is byte-identical to the pre-change output.
The full CI gate passes: templ generate leaves no diff, go vet ./... and go test ./... are clean, and CGO_ENABLED=0 GOOS=linux GOARCH=amd64 go build ./cmd/gringotts succeeds.
